fault_hypothesis: The overlap check on line 70 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), incorrectly allowing intervals that touch at their boundaries. The logic should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`) to correctly reject adjacent intervals as conflicts.

spec.expr: not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) or (end__1 < s or start__1 > e)

constraints.intent: If the code considers two intervals non-overlapping, it must be because they are strictly separate, not just touching.
An iteration should only complete if the new interval is strictly separate from the existing interval being checked.

spec.expr: all((interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e) for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the new interval is added, it must not have had a boundary conflict with any existing reservation.

spec.expr: not __cbfl_result[0] or all(__cbfl_result[1][i][1] < __cbfl_result[1][i+1][0] for i in range(len(__cbfl_result[1]) - 1))

constraints.intent: If a reservation is accepted, the resulting schedule must not contain any touching or overlapping intervals.
